By following the procedure described on page 19.

messages.content: You are a customer service agent for Canon printers.
How do you exit sleep mode on the machine?
Press the power button, tap the display, or press any key other than the power button.

provenance.chunk: When You Want to Set the Auto Sleep Timer (chunk 0)

chunk_text:
The factory default settings for the amount of time that elapses before the machine enters auto sleep mode are
listed belo w. We recommend using the factory default settings to save the most power. If you want to change
the amount of time that elapses before the machine enters auto sleep mode, follow the procedure below.

● 10 minutes (setting range is 3 to 60 minutes)

![When You Want to Set the Auto](images/image_p43_02.png)

<Menu> <Timer Settings> <Auto Sleep Time> Set the amount of time that elapses before the
machine automatically enters sleep mode <Apply>


![<Menu> <Timer Settings> <Auto Sleep Time> Set](images/image_p44_00.png)

**Exiting Sleep Mode**

Press . You can also tap the display or press any key other than to exit sleep mode.

messages.content: You are a Canon technical support specialist.
How do you disable the USB port located on the right side of the machine?
If a screen for entering a PIN is displayed, enter the PIN and tap <Apply>.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-024

You can disable the USB port located on the right side of the machine. If this port is disabled, the machine cannot
communicate with a computer via USB.

**● If a screen for entering a PIN is displayed, enter the PIN, and tap <Apply>. Setting the System Manager
PIN(P. 91)**

messages.content: You are a customer service agent for Canon printers.
How can the period of time after which auto sleep is performed be specified?
The period can be specified between 3 to 10 to 60 minutes.

chunk_text:
If the machine has not been operated or has had no data to process for a specified period of time, the machine enters
sleep mode to save energy consumption (Auto Sleep). Specify the period of time after which auto sleep is performed.
Auto Sleep is not performed when the setting menu is displayed. We recommend using the factory default settings to
Entering Sleep Mode(P. 39) save the most power.

3 to 10 to 60 (min.)

messages.content: You are a Canon printer hardware expert.
What should be specified for the Special Printing Mode setting when copying or printing on the reverse side of paper that has already been used?
<Off> should normally be specified for this setting.

chunk_text:
Paper jams may frequently occur when using the multi-purpose tray to copy/print on the reverse side of paper
that has already been copied/printed on. <Off> should normally be specified for this setting. Only change this
setting if the above symptom occurs.

![Special Printing Mode O](images/image_p114_01.png)

![Special Printing Mode O](images/image_p114_02.png)

<Menu> <Adjustment/Maintenance> <Special Processing> <Special Printing Mode

O> Select the mode

messages.content: You are a customer service agent for Canon printers.
What should be done if a printing job is queued when an original cannot be scanned due to insufficient memory space?
Wait until the job is completed.

chunk_text:
**An original could not be scanned due to insufficient memory space.**

● If a printing job is queued, wait until the job is completed.

**● If scanning, change the file format to reduce its file size.
Scanning from the Machine(P. 80)**

messages.content: You are a Canon printer hardware expert.
What should you do if you encounter problems when using the machine?
Check the items in the section before contacting us.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-02L

If you encounter problems when using the machine, check the items in this section before contacting us. If the
problems persist, contact your local authorized Canon dealer.

messages.content: You are a Canon printer hardware expert.
What happens to print quality if you continue printing after the machine displays a nearly empty toner message?
The print quality becomes poor.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-039

The machine will display a message when a toner cartridge is nearly empty. Note that the print quality becomes poor if
you continue printing without taking any action. You can check the remaining toner level on the display.

**When a Message Appear(P. 159)
If Printouts Are Poor in Quality(P. 159)
Checking Toner Level(P. 160)**

messages.content: You are a Canon printer hardware expert.
What should you do with purchase information that you no longer need?
Delete it.

chunk_text: Delete purchase information that you no longer need.

messages.content: You are a customer service agent for Canon printers.
How can I check the total number of pages used for printouts?
Select the item you want to check.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-03K

You can check total for the number of pages used for printouts. This total includes reports and lists, in addition to
copies and printouts of data from computers.

![Viewing the Counter Value](images/image_p177_00.png)

Select the item you want to check.

![Select the item you want](images/image_p177_01.png)

![Select the item you want](images/image_p177_02.png)


![Select the item you want](images/image_p177_03.png)


*1 The counted number per one printed/scanned sheet for each item on the counter is as follows:

"number in the Size (Large or Small) column" × "number in the Number of Printed/Scanned Sides (One-Sided or Two-Sided)
column"

Example: For large, single-sided print, the number added to <102: Total 2> is 2 × 1 = 2.

*2 Large: Standard size larger than A3/11" x 17".

*3 Small: Standard size smaller than B4/LGL.

messages.content: You are a Canon technical support specialist.
What should I do to enter sleep mode on this machine?
The machine will enter sleep mode automatically when not in use, or you can press the button to put it to sleep whenever you want; pressing it again instantly wakes the machine.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-041

![Going Green and Saving Money](images/image_p188_00.png)

![3EEJ-041](images/image_p188_01.png)

**Print Multiple Pages on a Single Sheet**

To really start saving paper, shrink multiple pages onto a single sheet - 2 or 4 pages for copies, and up to 16
pages for print jobs sent from your computer.

![To really start saving paper, shrink multiple](images/image_p188_02.png)

To copy multiple pages of a document onto one sheet: Copying Multiple Documents onto One Sheet (2 on
1)(P. 53) 1/4 on
To print multiple pages of a document onto one sheet: Printing from a Computer(P. 66)

![To copy multiple pages of a document](images/image_p188_03.png)

**Sleep Mode**

For saving energy and money, a sleep mode that powers down a machine when nobody is using it has become
an essential feature. This machine will enter sleep mode automatically, and can also be put to sleep whenever
once to put the machine to sleep. Press it again to instantly wake the machine back into you want. Press
action.

![For saving energy and money, a sleep](images/image_p188_04.png)

**For more information about this function, see Entering Sleep Mode(P. 39) .**

![For more information about this function, see](images/image_p188_05.png)

**Save Toner**

Got multi-page documents or drafts that don't need presentation-quality printing?


There's a setting that uses less toner. Give it a try and save toner and money.

![There's a setting that uses less toner.](images/image_p189_00.png)

**For more information about this function, see Printing from a Computer(P. 66) .**

messages.content: You are a Canon technical support specialist.
How can I save toner when printing multi-page documents or drafts?
There is a setting that uses less toner for documents or drafts that do not need presentation-quality printing; for more information, see 'Printing from a Computer' on page 66.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-041

![Going Green and Saving Money](images/image_p188_00.png)

![3EEJ-041](images/image_p188_01.png)

To really start saving paper, shrink multiple pages onto a single sheet - 2 or 4 pages for copies, and up to 16
pages for print jobs sent from your computer.

![To really start saving paper, shrink multiple](images/image_p188_02.png)

To copy multiple pages of a document onto one sheet: Copying Multiple Documents onto One Sheet (2 on
1)(P. 53) 1/4 on
To print multiple pages of a document onto one sheet: Printing from a Computer(P. 66)

![To copy multiple pages of a document](images/image_p188_03.png)

**Sleep Mode**

For saving energy and money, a sleep mode that powers down a machine when nobody is using it has become
an essential feature. This machine will enter sleep mode automatically, and can also be put to sleep whenever
once to put the machine to sleep. Press it again to instantly wake the machine back into you want. Press
action.

![For saving energy and money, a sleep](images/image_p188_04.png)

**For more information about this function, see Entering Sleep Mode(P. 39) .**

![For more information about this function, see](images/image_p188_05.png)

**Save Toner**

Got multi-page documents or drafts that don't need presentation-quality printing?


There's a setting that uses less toner. Give it a try and save toner and money.

![There's a setting that uses less toner.](images/image_p189_00.png)

**For more information about this function, see Printing from a Computer(P. 66) .**

messages.content: You are a Canon technical support specialist.
How can a user quickly find a specific page in the User's Guide?
The user can search from their intended use or enter a keyword.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-055

The User's Guide is a manual that can be viewed on your computer or mobile device and it describes all of the
functions of the machine. You can search from your intended use or enter a keyword to quickly find the page you are
looking for. The section describes the screen layout of the User's Guide and how to read the Guide. You can specify
how the appearance of the User's Guide looks.

messages.content: You are a Canon technical support specialist.
How do you search for pages containing an exact phrase?
Enclose the keywords in double quotation marks.

chunk_text:
Click to display the search window. Contains a text box to perform a search by keyword and find the page
are looking for. you


![Search](images/image_p210_00.png)

**[Enter keyword(s) here]**

Enter a keyword or keywords and click to display the search results. You can search for pages
keywords by separating the keywords by a space (blank). Also you can only search for containing all the
pages containing an exact phrase by enclosing the keywords in double quotation marks.

**Search result**

Displays the search results of the pages that contain the specified keywords. From the results, locate the
page you are looking for and click the topic title of the page.

keyword(s) are shown in bold in the search results. ● The specified

messages.content: You are a helpful Canon printer technician.
How can you cancel copying?
You can cancel copying by tapping <Cancel> on the screen, pressing the control panel immediately after pressing, or by checking the copy status.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-00X

If you want to cancel copying , tap <Cancel> on the screen or press on the immediately after pressing

control panel. You can also cancel copying after checking the copy status.

messages.content: You are a Canon technical support specialist.
How do you register frequently used copy settings?

To register frequently used copy settings, select <Copy>, then <Favorite Settings>, then <Regist./Edit>, choose <Settings 1> to <Settings 4>, select each setting and set the value, then select <Apply> and <Yes>.

chunk_text:
3EEJ-01A

You can register up to four frequently used combinations of copy settings. By simply selecting a registered setting
combination when it is needed, you can eliminate the need to configure the same settings every time you make
copies, such as if you frequently set the number of copies to 5, set <Erase Frame> to <On>, and increase the sharpness
when copying. You can register the setting combinations as <Settings 1> to <Settings 4>.

![Registering Frequently Used Copy Settings](images/image_p67_00.png)

<Copy> <Favorite Settings> <Regist./Edit> Select <Settings 1> to <Settings 4> Select each
setting and set the value <Apply> <Yes>

![<Copy> <Favorite Settings> <Regist./Edit> Select <Settings 1>](images/image_p67_01.png)

messages.content: You are a helpful Canon printer technician.
When should you select a lower mode number for Special Printing Mode M?
Select a lower mode number when printing on paper that has been stored at high temperature and high humidity for a long time.

chunk_text:
Change the <Special Printing Mode M> setting if there is a degradation in print quality or irregularity in print
density. This mode enables you to select one of five levels for the machine's transfer output. Select a higher
mode number if you want to print on paper to which toner does not adhere well, such as index cards. Select a
lower mode number when printing on paper that has been stored at high temperature and high humidity for a
long time.


![Special Printing Mode M](images/image_p115_00.png)

<Menu> <Adjustment/Maintenance> <Special Processing> <Special Printing Mode M>

Select the mode

Special Printing Mode N
Change the <Special Printing Mode N> setting if paper curls or jams when printing on paper that has absorbed
moisture. If <Mode 3> is set, the effect of this setting is maximized, but it may take the machine longer to print.

![Special Printing Mode N](images/image_p115_01.png)

![Special Printing Mode N](images/image_p115_02.png)

![Special Printing Mode N](images/image_p115_03.png)

<Menu> <Adjustment/Maintenance> <Special Processing> <Special Printing Mode N>

messages.content: You are a customer service agent for Canon printers.
When is it recommended to replace the toner cartridge?
It is recommended to replace the toner cartridge when printing a large volume of documents.

chunk_text:
**The toner cartridge needs to be replaced soon.**

**● It is recommended to replace the toner cartridge when printing a large volume of documents.
Replacing the Toner Cartridges(P. 159)**

messages.content: You are a helpful Canon printer technician.
How should paper be loaded into the printer?
Ensure the paper is properly loaded, verify that the number of sheets meets the paper requirements, and load the same size and type of paper into the paper drawer or multi-purpose tray.

provenance.chunk: Paper Is Not Fed, or Multiple Sheets of Paper Are Fed at a Time. (chunk 0)

chunk_text:
● Make sure that the paper is properly loaded.
Loading Paper(P. 20)
● Make sure that the number of paper you have loaded or paper to be used meets the paper requirements.
Loading Paper(P. 20)
● Make sure to load the same size and type of paper into the paper drawer or multi-purpose tray.

messages.content: You are a helpful Canon printer technician.
How can I check the level of remaining toner?
You can check the level of remaining toner by navigating to <Device Information> and selecting <Toner Level> to check the toner level.

chunk_text:
You can check the level of remaining toner according to the following, whenever you want to. It is recommended that
you check the level before you start to print a large job.

![◼ Toner Level Checking](images/image_p164_00.png)

<Device Information> <Toner Level> Check the toner level

![<Device Information> <Toner Level> Check the toner](images/image_p164_01.png)

You can check the machine settings by printing reports and lists.

registered User Data List/System Manager Data List(P. 171) in the machine.

**Copy/Print Charge Log Report
You can check the counter information. Copy/Print Charge Log Report(P. 172)**

What are the page numbers listed in the table?
You are a customer service agent for Canon printers.
What paper sizes are supported by tray Paper Drawer 1?

The supported paper sizes include Oficio (Brazil, Mexico, Argentina), Letter (Argentina, Government), LetterR (Argentina, Government), Legal (Government, India), Foolscap (Australia, standard), 8K, 16K, 16KR, F4A, Custom, and Envelopes (No.10/COM10, Monarch, ISO-C5, DL).
